all.critique: The beam search decoding description does not clearly specify how completed hypotheses (those that have generated the end‐of-sequence token) are managed separately from ongoing ones. Without detailing the mechanism to store finished hypotheses and integrate them properly in the final selection (including proper score normalization), the implementation may yield inaccurate results.

The high-level evaluation flow omits mentioning device management (e.g., ensuring that the model, input tensors, and outputs are moved to the correct device such as GPU or CPU). Failing to handle device placement can lead to performance issues or runtime errors.

The analysis assumes a fixed configuration structure (using config["inference"]["translation"] and config["inference"]["parsing"]) without discussing validation of these keys. It would be beneficial to verify or handle potential variations in the config file structure.

The description of the length penalty is somewhat generic. It mentions applying a multiplicative factor but does not provide the exact computation or normalization formula as used in the paper, which may hinder reproducibility.

The procedure for converting token indices back to text relies on an external tokenizer/vocabulary converter, yet no details are provided about handling edge cases such as subword tokens or token concatenation rules. Clarification here would ensure consistency with expected text output.
